title: X-ray Diffraction Crystal Space-Group Classification (PWDR)
sub_domain: Crystal symmetry classification from XRD lattice + structure-factor recovery
source_data_quality_note: v2 physical_with_discrete_readout (PWDR) genesis Principle. Wraps L1-518 XRD (newly authored alongside) with deterministic crystallographic-symmetry rules (Hermann-Mauguin notation, International Tables for Crystallography Vol A) to assign categorical space-group label from 230 possibilities. Universal in materials science, structural chemistry, pharma polymorph identification. UTSW structural biology programs.

gate_substitutions.conservation_substitute: softmax simplex closure on crystallographic-symmetry categorical readout: 230 space groups (or 14 Bravais lattices, 7 crystal systems, 32 point groups depending on resolution level)

discrete_readout.threshold_function: Space group(crystal) = decision-tree on recovered (a, b, c, alpha, beta, gamma, atomic_positions, systematic_absences) per International Tables for Crystallography Vol A: (1) Crystal system (7) from lattice parameter relations and angles: cubic if a=b=c & alpha=beta=gamma=90; tetragonal if a=b!=c & alpha=beta=gamma=90; etc. (2) Bravais lattice (14) from primitive/centered observations. (3) Point group (32) from systematic absences and Laue symmetry. (4) Space group (230) from glide planes / screw axes via systematic absences (e.g., absent (h00) for h=odd indicates 2_1 screw axis along a). Statistical N(z) test (Wilson plot) discriminates centrosymmetric vs non-centrosymmetric.
discrete_readout.underlying_physical_state: lattice parameters (a, b, c, alpha, beta, gamma), atomic positions, and systematic-absence pattern recovered by L1-518 XRD analytical core
discrete_readout.threshold_continuity_proof: Crystal system membership is determined by lattice-parameter relations (a vs b vs c, angles vs 90/120 degrees); each relation defines a measure-zero hyperplane. Bravais centering and space-group decisions are determined by systematic absence patterns — discrete tests on intensity ratios. Smoothed-classification (interpolated when lattice-parameter ratios are near boundaries) is globally Lipschitz with constant ~0.05 per percentage-point of lattice-parameter uncertainty. Inherits stability from L1-518.

related_principles: L1-031 (X-ray radiography — sibling X-ray modality, informational)
L1-294 (DFT — sibling first-principles structure prediction, informational)

E.description: X-ray Diffraction Space-Group Classification (PWDR): wraps L1-518 XRD analytical core with the deterministic crystallographic-symmetry decision tree from International Tables for Crystallography Vol A. Stage 1 (analytical, from L1-518): recover lattice parameters (a, b, c, alpha, beta, gamma), atomic positions, and systematic-absence pattern. Stage 2 (deterministic threshold): apply hierarchical decision tree (crystal system -> Bravais lattice -> point group -> space group) to assign one of 230 space groups per Hermann-Mauguin notation. Difficulty tier delta = 5. Mismatch parameters: lattice_parameter_uncertainty, systematic_absence_threshold_uncertainty, twinning_artifact, modulation_or_superlattice, pseudosymmetry_ambiguity.
E.forward_model: Stage 1 (L1-518): I(2*theta) -> (a, b, c, alpha, beta, gamma, atomic_positions, F_hkl). Stage 2: crystal_system = lattice_parameter_decision(a, b, c, angles); Bravais = centering_decision(systematic_absences); point_group = Laue_class_decision(F_hkl symmetries) + Wilson_plot(centrosymmetry); space_group = systematic_absence_decision(specific axes) per International Tables Vol A.
E.world_state_x: Per-crystal categorical space-group label in {1, 2, ..., 230} (or coarser: crystal system in {triclinic, ..., cubic}, Bravais in 14 lattices, point group in 32 groups).
E.observation_y: Powder XRD pattern I(2*theta) or single-crystal {I_hkl} (same as L1-518 core).

G.dag: L.xray_source -> L.bragg_diffraction -> L.structure_factor -> L.lattice_parameter_recovery -> L.systematic_absence_test -> L.space_group_decision_tree -> int.angular
coupling_constraints: lattice_to_symmetry: lattice parameters and systematic absences are jointly observed and jointly determine space-group membership; cannot separate fully (e.g., centrosymmetric vs non-centrosymmetric ambiguity in Laue class requires both lattice geometry and intensity statistics)

W.regime: Existence and uniqueness inherited from L1-518 with additional constraint: pseudosymmetry can cause space-group ambiguity (e.g., P2_1/n vs Pn — distinguishing requires additional statistics or chemistry constraint). Stability inherits L1-518's high condition number plus additive systematic_absence_threshold_uncertainty. Joint Hadamard well-posedness for the space-group classification established by Hahn 2005 (International Tables Vol A), Cromer-Liberman 1970 (anomalous scattering for centrosymmetry tests), Le Bail 2005 (powder indexing), Kabsch 2010 (XDS for space-group determination), Spek 2003 (PLATON ADDSYM).

C.solver_class: linear-operator + decision-tree [International Tables Vol A space-group decision tree] | direct methods + symmetry-search [Le Bail indexing + space-group enumeration] | linear-operator + deep neural prior [SpaceGroupNet]
C.error_bound: Inherits L1-518 lattice-parameter recovery bound. Misclassification probability bound: Pr(SG_hat != SG_true) <= C_thresh * |lattice_parameter_error| / d(parameters, system_boundary). Pseudosymmetry-induced bias adds + C_pseudo * pseudosymmetry_ambiguity_score.
C.complexity: O(N_reflections * 230) for full space-group enumeration; faster via hierarchical decision tree O(N_reflections * log(230))

recast_attempted.recast_path: Original framing: 'Classify XRD pattern into space group' (would be data_driven_statistical CNN-based pattern classifier). Option-3 recast: 'Recover lattice parameters and systematic-absence pattern via L1-518 Rietveld refinement; apply International Tables Vol A decision tree to assign space group from 230 possibilities'. Feasible because crystal symmetry has a well-defined diffraction-physics forward model and space-group decision rules are deterministic functions of recovered lattice geometry and absence patterns.

joint_well_posedness_references: Hahn T, ed. (2005) International Tables for Crystallography, Volume A: Space-group symmetry, 5th ed. International Union of Crystallography.
Cromer DT, Liberman D (1970) Relativistic calculation of anomalous scattering factors for X rays. J Chem Phys 53(5):1891-1898.
Le Bail A (2005) Powder pattern indexing — application to monocrystalline thin film PbS. Powder Diffraction 20(4):316-326.
Kabsch W (2010) XDS. Acta Crystallogr D Biol Crystallogr 66(2):125-132.
Spek AL (2003) Single-crystal structure validation with the program PLATON. J Appl Crystallogr 36(1):7-13.
Allmann R, Hinek R (2007) The introduction of structure types into the Inorganic Crystal Structure Database ICSD. Acta Crystallogr A 63(5):412-417.

distinctness_audit.rationale: L1-518 recovers continuous lattice parameters and structure factors; L1-519 wraps with International Tables decision tree to produce categorical space-group label. Different solution_space (1D space-group label vs continuous crystal-structure parameters); adds L.lattice_parameter_recovery, L.systematic_absence_test, L.space_group_decision_tree primitives.

v3_metadata.clinical_context: Beyond materials science academic research, space-group classification is critical in pharma polymorph identification (different polymorphs can have different bioavailability, solubility, stability — FDA-relevant), cement / metal alloy quality control, semiconductor characterization, and forensic mineralogy. Universal standard databases: Cambridge Structural Database (CSD, organic + organometallic), Inorganic Crystal Structure Database (ICSD), Protein Data Bank (PDB) — all use space-group classification as primary indexing. Reference solvers: PLATON ADDSYM, FINDSYM, ISOTROPY for symmetry detection and validation. UTSW has structural biology programs benefiting from space-group classification automation.
